source_item.title: Netflix shares tumble 13% after weak sales forecast, hit 22-month low

slide1_hook.label: Netflix Crashes to a 22-Month Low
slide1_hook.subhead: From record highs to a brutal selloff. What happened?

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: From Streaming King to Growth Doubts
slide2_backstory.body: Netflix hit an all time high in June 2025, powered by hits like Stranger Things and Squid Game. But its content slate weakened this year, and a failed bid for Warner Bros. Discovery raised doubts about future growth.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Weak Forecast Triggers a 13% Crash
slide3_news.body: Netflix's Q2 revenue hit $12.56 billion, up 13% and in line with estimates, but weak Q3 guidance spooked investors. Shares fell 13% to their lowest level since August 2024, now down 44% from last year's peak.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: Growth Story Under the Microscope
bullets: Trades at 20x earnings, so any slowdown hits hard
Cutting viewership reports spooked investors at a bad time
Ad revenue is still just $3B of $51B

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Netflix is betting its future on ads and live sports while sharing less data on viewership. Is its high stock price still justified, or has the easy money already been made?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for daily business stories explained simply. Drop your take in the comments 👇
